title: Claude: Write, Research, Build
modules: What Claude Does Differently (and Why It Matters)
Writing With Claude: Essays, Emails, and Side Projects
Research Mode: Summarizing, Synthesizing, and Fact-Checking
Building Something Real: Projects, Pitches, and Portfolios
Where Claude Falls Short and How to Work Around It
synopsis: You've probably heard of Claude but maybe haven't figured out how to make it actually useful for your life â this course fixes that fast. You'll go from casual user to someone who uses Claude to produce real output: better writing, sharper research, and projects you can show people.
rationale: Claude is rapidly gaining traction among students and knowledge workers but lacks dedicated beginner-friendly guidance that connects it to real academic and side-project use cases.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can use Claude to draft, research, and refine work across writing-heavy tasks â and know exactly when to trust it and when to push back.
source_signals: Opus 4.5 is not the normal AI agent experience that I have had thus far
